comment: Scaffold for Routines V1 work. Bodies are the deck-canonical specs per omp-deck convention (tasks live in the deck; my-org-new stubs are forwarding pointers). Companion plan: docs/proposals/routines-v1-plan.md. Phases: P0 Foundation (3 tasks, 1 wk), P1 Engine (5 tasks, 2 wk), P2 Daily-briefing (1 task, 0.5 wk), P3 Visual builder + observability (5 tasks, 3 wk), V1 ship (1 task). Total ~6.5 weeks.

tasks.title: [V1 P0] DB migration 003-routines-v1 + protocol type extensions
tasks.body:
## Phase
P0 - Foundation

## Scope
Lay the data-model foundation for multi-step routines. Per docs/proposals/routines-v1-plan.md section 6.1.

## Approach
- `apps/server/src/db/migrations/003-routines-v1.sql`:
  - ALTER `routines`: add `spec_yaml TEXT`, `concurrency TEXT NOT NULL DEFAULT 'skip'`, `budget_json TEXT`, `tags TEXT`, `timezone TEXT`, `spec_version INTEGER NOT NULL DEFAULT 0`
  - ALTER `routine_runs`: add `trigger_kind TEXT`, `trigger_payload TEXT`, `total_llm_tokens INTEGER NOT NULL DEFAULT 0`, `total_llm_cost_micros INTEGER NOT NULL DEFAULT 0`, `aborted_at TEXT`, `abort_reason TEXT`, `step_count_total INTEGER NOT NULL DEFAULT 0`, `step_count_failed INTEGER NOT NULL DEFAULT 0`
  - Rebuild `routine_runs` with relaxed `trigger` CHECK constraint (SQLite ALTER CHECK requires table swap)
  - CREATE `routine_step_runs` (id, run_id FK CASCADE, step_id, step_index, step_type, started_at, ended_at, status, stdout_excerpt, stderr_excerpt, output_json, error, model, llm_tokens_in, llm_tokens_out, llm_cost_micros, duration_ms, attempt) + indexes on (run_id, step_index) and (step_id, started_at DESC)
  - CREATE `routine_webhook_secrets` (routine_id PK FK CASCADE, path UNIQUE, secret_hash, created_at, last_used_at)
  - CREATE `routine_state` (routine_id FK CASCADE, key, value_json, updated_at; PK on routine_id+key)
  - NO `mcp_servers` or `integrations` table - per plan, MCP state comes from the SDK's MCP client; defer the table to V1.5 if richer dashboard state is needed
- `packages/protocol/src/index.ts`: add types `RoutineSpec`, `Step` (discriminated union by `type`), `Trigger` (discriminated union by kind), `RoutineStepRun`, `RoutineConcurrency`, `RoutineBudget`, plus WS frame types `RoutineRunStarted`, `RoutineStepEvent`, `RoutineRunFinished`. Existing `Routine`, `RoutineActionKind`, `RoutineRun` types remain (V0 spec_version=0 routines continue working unchanged).

## Acceptance
- Migration applies cleanly to fresh DB AND to an existing V0 routines DB without data loss
- V0 spec_version=0 routines table queries (existing tests, existing routes) still pass unchanged
- New protocol types compile; ts-no-implicit-any across the workspace
- Schema diff documented in migration header comment

## Dependencies
None (root of P0)

tasks.title: [V1 P0] JSON schemas for routine spec validation (Ajv) + per-step-type schemas
tasks.body:
## Phase
P0 - Foundation

## Scope
Author the JSON Schemas that are the single source of truth for both Phase 1 (Ajv runtime validation) and Phase 3 (visual builder form rendering). Per docs/proposals/routines-v1-plan.md section 2 + the cross-cutting note at end of section 8.6.

## Approach
- New directory `packages/protocol/src/schemas/`
- Common-fields schema `step-common.json`: `id` (required string), `when?` (string), `on_failure` (enum abort|continue|retry, default abort), `retry?` ({times, backoff, max_delay_secs}), `timeout_secs?` (number)
- One schema per step type, each `allOf`-referencing step-common.json:
  - `step-run.json`: command (string), cwd? (string)
  - `step-agent.json`: prompt (string), model? (string), structured_output? ({schema, strict}), skills_allowed? (string[]), mcp_servers_allowed? (string[])
  - `step-write.json`: path (string), content (string), append? (bool)
  - `step-http.json`: method (enum GET|POST|...), url (string), headers? (object), query? (object), body? (any), expect_json? (bool)
  - `step-mcp.json`: server (string), tool (string), args? (object)
  - `step-transform.json`: body (string; JS expression source)
  - `step-wait.json`: duration_secs (number)
  - `step-set_state.json`: state (object key->value)
- Trigger schemas: `trigger-cron.json` (cron string), `trigger-webhook.json` ({path, secret_env}), `trigger-manual.json` ({params_schema?: object}), `trigger-event.json` ({source, type?, filter?})
- Wrapper `routine-spec.json`: name, description?, trigger (array of trigger union), concurrency, timezone?, budget?, state? ({declared_keys?: string[]}), tags? (string[]), steps (array of step union)
- Export typed Ajv validator from `packages/protocol/src/validate.ts`: `validateRoutineSpec(spec): {valid: bool, errors: AjvError[]}`
- Validate every example in the V1 plan (sections 4.2, 4.4) and at least three intentional-malformed cases

## Acceptance
- `validateRoutineSpec` accepts the daily-briefing spec from plan section 4.2 verbatim
- `validateRoutineSpec` accepts the inbox-triager spec from plan section 4.4 verbatim
- Three crafted malformed specs (missing required field, wrong step-type discriminator, invalid cron) fail with line-pointing error messages
- Schemas are reachable from both apps/server (Ajv) and apps/web (the visual builder will consume the same files)

## Dependencies
[V1 P0] DB migration 003-routines-v1 + protocol type extensions

tasks.title: [V1 P0] Templating engine + sandboxed JS evaluator for when/transform
tasks.body:
## Phase
P0 - Foundation

## Scope
The `{{ expr }}` template engine and safe-JS evaluation primitives that every step type relies on. Per docs/proposals/routines-v1-plan.md section 2.4 + section 10.2 sandbox requirements.

## Approach
- `apps/server/src/routines/template.ts`:
  - Handlebars-style `{{ path.to.value }}` parser walking the context object (run, trigger, steps, env, secrets, state)
  - Whole-value substitution: when the expression resolves to an object/array, the result is the value itself (not its stringification) - important for downstream JSON payloads
  - Helper functions: `{{ value | json }}`, `{{ value | length }}`
  - String coercion is explicit (no implicit toString on objects); leaving expressions in their natural form helps the rendered YAML stay round-trippable
- `apps/server/src/routines/sandbox.ts`:
  - Wrap quickjs-emscripten (preferred over vm2 which has known CVEs)
  - Per-call setup: fresh QuickJS runtime, freeze the `context` object into the global scope, return the evaluated expression value
  - Hard wall-clock limit 100ms (use QuickJS interrupt handler)
  - No `require`, no `process`, no `fetch`, no timers > 100ms; verify by attempting each in unit tests
  - Secret-redaction: when the evaluator returns a value, scrub any string that matches a known secret value (cross-reference `context.secrets`) before returning - protects against accidental secret echo in transform output
- Unit tests: 12+ cases covering template substitution, helper functions, sandbox refusals, sandbox timeout, secret redaction

## Acceptance
- `render('{{ run.id }}', ctx)` returns the run id string
- `render('{{ steps.fetch.json }}', ctx)` returns the underlying object (not '[object Object]')
- `evaluate('1 + 1', ctx)` returns 2
- `evaluate('require("fs")', ctx)` throws
- `evaluate('while(true){}', ctx)` throws within 150ms with a timeout error
- `evaluate('context.secrets.MY_KEY', ctx)` returns 'REDACTED' or similar marker, not the actual value

## Dependencies
[V1 P0] DB migration 003-routines-v1 + protocol type extensions

tasks.title: [V1 P1] Multi-step routine runner core (run/agent/write/transform/wait/set_state)
tasks.body:
## Phase
P1 - Engine

## Scope
Rewrite `apps/server/src/routines-runner.ts` to execute multi-step pipelines with the 6 step types that don't require external integrations. http and mcp land in separate tasks for clean isolation.

## Approach
- Per-step executors at `apps/server/src/routines/steps/{run,agent,write,transform,wait,set_state}.ts`. Each exports `execute(step, ctx): Promise<StepResult>`.
- Runner core at `apps/server/src/routines-runner.ts`:
  - Initialize context from trigger payload, state (read all routine_state rows), env, secrets
  - For each step in spec order:
    1. Evaluate `when:` (via sandbox); if false, write skipped step_run record, continue
    2. Insert routine_step_runs row with status='running'
    3. Broadcast routine_step_event WS frame
    4. Execute step body with timeout (per-step or default), retry policy
    5. Capture stdout/stderr/structured output; update context
    6. Update routine_step_runs with final status, output, duration
    7. Broadcast routine_step_event WS frame
    8. Check budget (token sum, wall clock, step count); abort if exceeded
  - Finalize routine_runs row
  - Broadcast routine_run_finished
- `on_failure: abort | continue | retry` semantics. `retry: { times, backoff: linear|exponential, max_delay_secs }` with exponential default
- Per-step `timeout_secs` defaults: 60 for non-agent, 600 for agent
- Routine-level `timeout_secs` global cap (default 1800)
- Old single-action V0 path stays put for spec_version=0 routines; runner branches on spec_version at start of each invocation

## Acceptance
- A 5-step routine with mixed types (transform, write, agent, set_state, write) executes end-to-end, persisting one routine_step_runs row per step
- Failure on step 3 with `on_failure: abort` causes steps 4+ to NOT execute and the routine_runs row to be marked failed; partial output preserved
- Failure on step 3 with `on_failure: continue` allows steps 4+ to proceed; `steps.3.status` is 'failed' and downstream `when:` can branch on it
- Retry with `times: 3, backoff: exponential` retries with 1s/2s/4s gaps; final failure if all fail
- WS frames arrive at clients in real-time during a long-running routine
- V0 spec_version=0 routines continue to run via the old path; existing routine_runs page renders them correctly

## Dependencies
[V1 P0] DB migration 003-routines-v1 + protocol type extensions
[V1 P0] JSON schemas for routine spec validation (Ajv) + per-step-type schemas
[V1 P0] Templating engine + sandboxed JS evaluator for when/transform

tasks.title: [V1 P1] HTTP step type + internal routine-runner bearer token
tasks.body:
## Phase
P1 - Engine

## Scope
Add the `http` step type and the auth shim that lets a routine call the deck's own REST API without a user session.

## Approach
- `apps/server/src/routines/steps/http.ts`:
  - Use `fetch` (Bun native); apply method, url (after templating), headers (after templating), query (URLSearchParams), body (after templating; JSON-serialize if Content-Type is application/json or not set)
  - `expect_json: true` parses response body as JSON and captures to `steps.X.json`; otherwise stdout is the text body
  - Capture response status to `steps.X.status` (number)
  - Default timeout_secs: 60
- Internal auth shim:
  - Routine-runner mints a per-run HMAC token: `HMAC-SHA256(secret_key, runId)` truncated to 16 bytes hex
  - Secret key lives in env `DECK_INTERNAL_RUNNER_SECRET` (generated on first boot if missing; persisted to env-store)
  - Outgoing http steps to localhost:* automatically inject header `X-Routine-Internal-Auth: <token>` and `X-Routine-Run-Id: <runId>`
  - New auth middleware at `apps/server/src/middleware/internal-auth.ts`: checks the header; if present and HMAC valid for the claimed runId, bypasses session check; logs the route accessed for audit
  - Calls to non-localhost URLs do NOT get the internal header (preserves zero-trust outside the deck)

## Acceptance
- An http step in the daily-briefing routine calls `GET http://localhost:8787/api/tasks?state=done&since=24h` and captures the response in `steps.X.json`
- A request to a non-deck URL (e.g. example.com) does NOT include the X-Routine-Internal-Auth header (proven by request-mock test)
- A forged X-Routine-Internal-Auth header (wrong HMAC) returns 401 from the internal-auth middleware
- Token rotation: a token minted for run A is rejected when used in a request claiming run B

## Dependencies
[V1 P1] Multi-step routine runner core (run/agent/write/transform/wait/set_state)

tasks.title: [V1 P1] MCP step type + mcp_servers_allowed wiring to SDK MCP client
tasks.body:
## Phase
P1 - Engine

## Scope
Add the `mcp` step type for deterministic tool calls + `mcp_servers_allowed` on agent steps for restricting the LLM tool surface. Per docs/proposals/routines-v1-plan.md section 5.4.

## Approach
- `apps/server/src/routines/steps/mcp.ts`:
  - Resolve `step.server` against the SDK's MCP client registry (via the in-process bridge's `loadCapability` for the mcp capability)
  - Fetch the cached tool list for that server; find the tool matching `step.tool`
  - Validate `step.args` (after templating) against the tool's `inputSchema` using Ajv
  - Invoke `tool.call(args)` via the MCP client; await response; capture as `steps.X.json`
  - If the server is not installed or the tool not found, error with a clear message naming what's available
- Agent step extension (`apps/server/src/routines/steps/agent.ts`):
  - When `step.mcp_servers_allowed` is set, pass the filtered list to the agent session's MCP client config so the agent only sees those servers' tools at the LLM
  - When `step.skills_allowed` is set, similarly restrict the native skill surface
  - Both defaults: no restriction (all installed)
- Documentation snippet at top of mcp.ts: how `/mcp install` interacts with this step type; pointer to plan section 5

## Acceptance
- With a test MCP server installed via `/mcp install` in chat, an `mcp` step calls one of its tools and the response lands in `steps.X.json`
- An agent step with `mcp_servers_allowed: []` cannot call any MCP tool even when multiple servers are installed (LLM's tool surface is empty for MCP)
- An agent step with `mcp_servers_allowed: ["foo"]` can see foo's tools but not bar's
- An `mcp` step referencing a non-installed server errors with `server 'X' not installed; installed: [...]`

## Dependencies
[V1 P1] Multi-step routine runner core (run/agent/write/transform/wait/set_state)

tasks.title: [V1 P1] Trigger router: webhook receiver + manual run + multi-cron
tasks.body:
## Phase
P1 - Engine

## Scope
New trigger types beyond cron + extend cron to allow multiple cron entries per routine. Per docs/proposals/routines-v1-plan.md section 2.3.

## Approach
- Multi-trigger support in the routine spec parser: `trigger:` is always an array (even if length 1); each entry is a discriminated union
- Croner scheduler updates: walk all routines, for each `cron` trigger entry, schedule independently; non-cron triggers are passive (no scheduler action)
- Webhook receiver:
  - `apps/server/src/routes-hooks.ts`: Hono router mounted at `/hooks/*`
  - Lookup the routine by the path slug against `routine_webhook_secrets`
  - Verify `X-Routine-Signature: sha256=<hex>` header (HMAC-SHA256 of raw body, secret from routine_webhook_secrets.secret_hash via Argon2 verify)
  - Reject with 401 if signature is missing or invalid; log a row in routine_runs with `trigger_kind='webhook'` and `abort_reason='signature_invalid'` so brute-force attempts are visible
  - On success, enqueue a routine run with `trigger_payload` = parsed body
- Manual run endpoint:
  - `POST /api/routines/:id/run` body `{ params? }`
  - Validates `params` against `trigger.manual.params_schema` if defined; rejects with 400 if mismatch
  - Returns `{ runId }` immediately; run executes async
- Secret management:
  - `POST /api/routines/:id/webhook-secret/rotate` returns a new secret (one-time display); stores Argon2id hash + sets `path` slug
  - Secret format: 32 random bytes base64url
- Trigger payload available at `context.trigger.X` in every step

## Acceptance
- A routine with `trigger: [{cron: "0 7 * * *"}, {webhook: {path: "/hooks/test", secret_env: "TEST_SECRET"}}]` fires both on cron and on POST to /hooks/test
- POST to /hooks/test with bad HMAC returns 401, no run started, audit row logged
- POST to /hooks/test with good HMAC returns 202 + runId
- Manual run via API fires immediately and returns the runId
- A second `routine_finished` event source NOT implemented yet (deferred per plan)

## Dependencies
[V1 P1] Multi-step routine runner core (run/agent/write/transform/wait/set_state)

tasks.title: [V1 P1] Concurrency controller + budget enforcer + cross-run state
tasks.body:
## Phase
P1 - Engine

## Scope
The safety primitives that keep routines from each other and from themselves. Per docs/proposals/routines-v1-plan.md sections 2.6, 2.7, 2.8.

## Approach
- Concurrency controller at `apps/server/src/routines/concurrency.ts`:
  - In-memory Map<routineId, RunningRun[]> tracking active runs
  - Policy `skip` (default): if a run is in flight, drop the new trigger and log to routine_runs with `abort_reason='concurrency_skipped'`
  - Policy `queue`: queue subsequent invocations, max depth 10; over that, drop
  - Policy `cancel-previous`: signal the in-flight run to abort cleanly (sets a flag the runner checks between steps), start new run; old run's routine_runs row updated to `abort_reason='cancelled'`
  - Policy `parallel: N`: allow up to N concurrent; over that, behave per `overflow:` setting (default skip)
- Budget enforcer at `apps/server/src/routines/budget.ts`:
  - Accumulators per run: tokens_in, tokens_out, cost_micros, wall_clock_ms, steps_executed
  - Checked between each step (and inside long-running agent steps via the SDK's token-stream callback if available)
  - On excess: abort the run with `abort_reason='budget'` and the specific limit hit (e.g., 'budget:max_llm_cost_usd')
  - Cost computed via model-price table at `apps/server/src/budgets/model-prices.ts` (see plan section 10.4)
- Cross-run state:
  - `set_state` step executor writes to `routine_state` table atomically (UPSERT on (routine_id, key))
  - State read at the START of each run: `SELECT key, value_json FROM routine_state WHERE routine_id = ?`, materialized into `context.state.<key>`
  - State persisted only on successful step; aborted/failed runs do not write state (transactional boundary at the step level)

## Acceptance
- Two concurrent triggers on the same routine with `concurrency: skip` result in only one run; second is logged as skipped
- `concurrency: cancel-previous` test: long-running routine cancels mid-flight when re-triggered; new run starts; old run's routine_runs row marked cancelled
- `concurrency: queue` test: 3 rapid triggers result in 3 sequential runs, completed in order
- Budget overrun on `max_llm_cost_usd` aborts the run between steps with the right reason
- `set_state` on run N is visible as `context.state.X` on run N+1
- Run aborted with `abort_reason='budget'` does NOT write state

## Dependencies
[V1 P1] Multi-step routine runner core (run/agent/write/transform/wait/set_state)

tasks.title: [V1 P2] Daily-briefing routine + install flow + smoke test
tasks.body:
## Phase
P2 - Proof point

## Scope
Ship the V1 proof-point routine end-to-end. This is the demo that proves the engine works. Per docs/proposals/routines-v1-plan.md section 4.

## Approach
- Author the `daily-briefing` routine spec at `apps/server/src/templates/daily-briefing.yaml` (verbatim from plan section 4.2)
- 'Install Daily Briefing' affordance:
  - On routines list page, add an 'Install a starter routine' button -> modal listing available templates (V1 has just daily-briefing)
  - Click 'Install': POSTs the parsed spec_yaml to `/api/routines`, returns the routine in disabled state, scrolls to it in the list
- Smoke test runbook at `docs/proposals/daily-briefing-smoke-test.md`:
  1. Install via the button
  2. Enable; verify next-run-at shows 7am Chicago tomorrow
  3. Manual run -> verify steps execute in order, each shows green in the run detail view
  4. Confirm `inbox/captures/morning-briefing-YYYY-MM-DD.md` lands with all four sections (shipped/in-flight/needs-attention/top-focus)
  5. Confirm `last_briefing_date` written to routine_state
  6. Re-run manually within the same day -> verify the should_run step short-circuits, subsequent steps marked skipped, no duplicate file write
- E2E test in `apps/server/test/daily-briefing.test.ts`

## Acceptance
- Install button works on a cold deck
- Manual run produces a real briefing using the active model on a tenant with real tasks/inbox/runs
- Idempotency: re-running the same day skips downstream steps as expected
- Cron next-fire shown correctly in the routine card
- Smoke test runbook validates against a fresh install

## Dependencies
[V1 P1] Multi-step routine runner core (run/agent/write/transform/wait/set_state)
[V1 P1] HTTP step type + internal routine-runner bearer token
[V1 P1] Trigger router: webhook receiver + manual run + multi-cron
[V1 P1] Concurrency controller + budget enforcer + cross-run state

tasks.title: [V1 P3] Per-step form components for 8 step types + StepCommonFields
tasks.body:
## Phase
P3 - Visual builder + observability

## Scope
Form-mode rendering for each step type, driven by the JSON schemas from P0. Per docs/proposals/routines-v1-plan.md section 8.6 Tier 1.

## Approach
- Shared component `apps/web/src/components/routine-builder/StepCommonFields.tsx`:
  - Renders id (string input), when (string textarea with monospace), on_failure (dropdown), retry (collapsible: times/backoff/max_delay_secs), timeout_secs (number)
- One step-specific form per type at `apps/web/src/components/routine-builder/steps/{Run,Agent,Write,Http,Mcp,Transform,Wait,SetState}.tsx`:
  - Reads JSON Schema from `packages/protocol/src/schemas/`
  - Renders fields via @rjsf/core with deck-themed widget overrides (engineer's-notebook aesthetic per architecture.md)
  - Agent step: model dropdown reads `ModelRegistry` via existing settings hooks; `structured_output.schema` opens a JSON Schema mini-editor in a side panel; `skills_allowed` / `mcp_servers_allowed` are multi-select chip inputs reading from `/api/skills` / `/api/mcp/servers`
  - Mcp step (V1): `server` and `tool` are text inputs with autocomplete from `/api/mcp/servers` (Tier 2 upgrades to dropdowns)
  - Write step: path input + content textarea (monospace, full-width); append toggle
  - Http step: method dropdown, URL, headers/query (key-value table), body textarea (with content-type switch), expect_json toggle
  - Transform step: body is a small Monaco editor (JS mode); 'Reset' button restores from last saved
- Theme overrides at `apps/web/src/components/routine-builder/rjsf-theme.tsx`
- Storybook (or equivalent) entry per form for visual review

## Acceptance
- Every step type's form renders all schema fields
- Field-level validation errors surface inline with the deck's existing error styling
- Saving a form produces YAML that re-validates clean against the same schema
- Theme matches the rest of the deck (no @rjsf default blue)

## Dependencies
[V1 P0] DB migration 003-routines-v1 + protocol type extensions
[V1 P0] JSON schemas for routine spec validation (Ajv) + per-step-type schemas

tasks.title: [V1 P3] YAML <-> form round-tripping + add-step picker + up/down reorder
tasks.body:
## Phase
P3 - Visual builder + observability

## Scope
The bidirectional sync that makes the Builder tab feel coherent. Per docs/proposals/routines-v1-plan.md section 8.6 Tier 1 + section 11 open question 2.

## Approach
- Use `yaml` npm package's `Document` API to preserve comments and key order on round-trip (recommended option (b) from plan section 11)
- State model: single source-of-truth is the parsed YAML Document; form mode renders fields by walking the document tree; YAML mode renders the same tree stringified
- Form change -> mutate the corresponding YAML node in place, preserving surrounding comments
- YAML change (valid) -> reparse and re-render form; if structurally identical, only update changed fields (prevents form-state thrash)
- YAML change (invalid) -> form view disables with an inline error card showing line number + parse error; YAML editor stays editable
- Add-step picker:
  - Floating + button at end of steps list opens a dropdown of step types with one-line descriptions
  - Selecting inserts a scaffold YAML block at the end of `steps:`, with `id: <type>_<idx>` and required fields populated with placeholders
- Up/down reorder: arrow buttons on each step card swap the step with its neighbor in the YAML doc
- Smart reorder warning: scan step references; if reordering breaks a `steps.X.json` reference (a step that references X is now ABOVE X), surface an inline warning with a one-click undo. Lifted into V1 from plan section 8.6 Tier 2 because the smart-reorder check is cheap once we have the AST.

## Acceptance
- Edit a field in form mode -> YAML buffer updates correctly, preserving comments above the edit
- Edit YAML directly -> form back-populates
- Introduce invalid YAML -> form disables with line-pointing error
- Add-step picker inserts scaffolds that validate against the schema
- Up/down reorder preserves all other formatting
- Reordering step B above step A when B references steps.A.json -> warning shown with revert button

## Dependencies
[V1 P3] Per-step form components for 8 step types + StepCommonFields

tasks.title: [V1 P3] Trigger picker + routine settings form
tasks.body:
## Phase
P3 - Visual builder + observability

## Scope
Non-step parts of the routine spec: triggers and routine-level settings. Per docs/proposals/routines-v1-plan.md section 8.6 Tier 1.

## Approach
- Trigger picker at `apps/web/src/components/routine-builder/triggers/`:
  - `CronTriggerForm.tsx`: reuse the V0 cron expression picker (already implements next-5-runs preview); extended to allow multiple cron entries per routine
  - `WebhookTriggerForm.tsx`: path input + secret regenerate button (calls `POST /api/routines/:id/webhook-secret/rotate`) + copy-to-clipboard; show the full webhook URL
  - `ManualTriggerForm.tsx`: JSON Schema mini-editor for `params_schema` (the manual-run dashboard form derives from this)
  - `EventTriggerForm.tsx`: source dropdown (telegram, deck_inbox, deck_task), type/filter inputs as applicable
  - Container component lists all triggers and lets user add/remove (each trigger is a row with type-specific form inline)
- Routine settings form at `apps/web/src/components/routine-builder/RoutineSettingsForm.tsx`:
  - Name (string)
  - Description (textarea)
  - Concurrency (dropdown: skip/queue/cancel-previous/parallel; parallel reveals an N input)
  - Timezone (picker with common zones surfaced + search for the rest)
  - Budget (collapsible: max_duration_secs, max_llm_cost_usd, max_steps_executed)
  - Declared state keys (multi-tag input; type a key, press enter to add)
  - Tags (multi-tag input)
- Settings tab in the routine detail view layout: drops these forms in a stack with section dividers

## Acceptance
- Add a webhook trigger to an existing cron routine; both fire (verified via the existing E2E test from P1 trigger router task)
- Cron preview is accurate for the chosen timezone
- Manual params schema editor produces a schema that the manual-run dashboard uses
- Settings form persists all fields; reload preserves them

## Dependencies
[V1 P0] DB migration 003-routines-v1 + protocol type extensions

tasks.title: [V1 P3] Run detail view: live WS timeline + replay + debug mode
tasks.body:
## Phase
P3 - Visual builder + observability

## Scope
The new /runs/:id page customers use to debug routines. Per docs/proposals/routines-v1-plan.md section 8.3.

## Approach
- New route `/routines/:routineId/runs/:runId` at `apps/web/src/views/RunDetailView.tsx`
- Header: routine name, run id, trigger kind (with payload preview popover), status pill, duration, total cost in micros (display as USD with 4 decimals)
- Timeline: vertical strip of step cards, each:
  - Status icon (running/success/skipped/failed/aborted)
  - Step id, step_type pill
  - Duration (formatted with ms precision under 1s, otherwise human-readable)
  - For agent steps: model + token counts (in/out)
  - Expand: show stdout, stderr, output_json (JSON pretty-printer), error details
- Live updates: subscribe to WS frames `routine_run_started`, `routine_step_event`, `routine_run_finished` filtered by runId; update timeline in place as events arrive
- Buttons:
  - 'Replay': POST `/api/routines/:id/replay/:runId` -> redirect to new run detail page
  - 'Re-run': POST `/api/routines/:id/run` with empty params
  - 'Open in chat': fork a chat session preloaded with run context (paste the routine spec + last-run output as the opener)
  - 'Cancel': only visible when status is 'running'; POST `/api/routines/:id/runs/:runId/cancel`
  - 'Debug mode' toggle: only on Re-run; mints a debug run with verbose logging + longer stdout retention
- Permission for the cancel endpoint: routine-owner only (V1 is single-user so no check needed; future-proof comment)

## Acceptance
- A live run shows step-by-step progression in real time over WS
- Replay creates a new run that the timeline shows; original run remains visible in history
- Debug run retains untruncated stdout/stderr (subject to 1MB ceiling)
- 'Open in chat' creates a session with the routine spec in the system prompt

## Dependencies
[V1 P1] Multi-step routine runner core (run/agent/write/transform/wait/set_state)
[V1 P1] Trigger router: webhook receiver + manual run + multi-cron

tasks.title: [V1 P3] Spec tab (Monaco) + metrics on routine card + integrations nav stub
tasks.body:
## Phase
P3 - Visual builder + observability

## Scope
Final UI polish making V1 ship-ready. Per docs/proposals/routines-v1-plan.md section 8.2 + 8.4.

## Approach
- Spec tab at `apps/web/src/components/routine-builder/SpecTab.tsx`:
  - Monaco YAML editor wired to the same backing `yaml.Document` as Builder tab (single source of truth)
  - Schema validation via Ajv (the same validator from P0); errors render as red squiggles with hover tooltips
  - Lint markers for common issues: undeclared state key reference, broken `steps.X.json` reference, malformed cron, unknown step type
  - 'Diff against saved' button: side-by-side diff view (Monaco's native diff editor) between current buffer and last-saved spec
- Metrics aggregation:
  - Endpoint `GET /api/routines/:id/metrics` returns `{ total: number, success_rate_30d: number, p50_duration_ms: number, p95_duration_ms: number, mtd_cost_micros: number }`
  - Service `apps/server/src/routines/metrics.ts` does the aggregation via SQL (no separate metrics store; just `SELECT` over routine_runs + routine_step_runs)
  - Surfaced on each routine card in the list: success sparkline (inline SVG from last 30 outcomes), MTD cost in dollars with 4 decimals
- Integrations nav stub:
  - Sidebar entry 'Integrations' with a 'V1.5' badge
  - Page `apps/web/src/views/IntegrationsStubView.tsx`: empty state with copy 'Coming in V1.5. For now, install MCP servers via `/mcp install` in chat (see the omp marketplace).' + link to docs/proposals/routines-v1-plan.md section 5 for the curated list

## Acceptance
- Spec tab lints `daily-briefing.yaml` with no false positives; deliberately broken specs show squiggles
- Diff view renders correctly for a small change
- Routine card shows live metrics after a routine has run at least once
- Integrations nav stub doesn't 404; links work

## Dependencies
[V1 P0] JSON schemas for routine spec validation (Ajv) + per-step-type schemas
[V1 P3] Per-step form components for 8 step types + StepCommonFields

tasks.title: [V1 ship] V1 release: docs, changelog, tutorial, marketing screenshot
tasks.body:
## Phase
V1 ship gate

## Scope
Final V1 cutover. Everything that turns 'engine works on my machine' into 'a user can install and use it'.

## Approach
- Update root `CHANGELOG.md` with V1 entry: link the 3 KB articles created during V1 buildout, list breaking changes (V0 routines auto-migrate; no action needed), credit deferred items going to V1.5
- Author `docs/routines-v1-tutorial.md`:
  - 'Install Daily Briefing in 60 seconds' walkthrough
  - Screenshots of the visual builder (form mode, YAML mode, run detail view)
  - Common gotchas (timezone, secret rotation, budget caps)
  - 'What's next in V1.5' teaser linking to the Workspace MCP plan
- Update `README.md` with one-paragraph routines pitch + link to the tutorial
- Marketing-quality screenshot of the visual builder for use in the omp-deck README + landing site if applicable
- Tag the release `v1.0.0` (or whatever fits omp-deck's versioning); push tags
- Verify the daily-briefing smoke-test runbook works against the tagged release on a fresh install
- Capture any V1 retrospective notes to `inbox/captures/routines-v1-retro-YYYY-MM-DD.md` for the V1.5 kickoff

## Acceptance
- Tutorial reads end-to-end without requiring internal context
- Smoke test passes on a fresh install of the tagged release
- CHANGELOG entry merged
- Tag pushed to origin
- Retro captured

## Dependencies
[V1 P2] Daily-briefing routine + install flow + smoke test
[V1 P3] Per-step form components for 8 step types + StepCommonFields
[V1 P3] YAML <-> form round-tripping + add-step picker + up/down reorder
[V1 P3] Trigger picker + routine settings form
[V1 P3] Run detail view: live WS timeline + replay + debug mode
[V1 P3] Spec tab (Monaco) + metrics on routine card + integrations nav stub
